published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovEtAl2018ECTJ and ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022ADML: ATE orthogonal/Riesz-score EIF baselines

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_dml_orth_uniform_v1 only gives weak-overlap studentization projection indices, not a fixed-law LRO normalization iff frontier

published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovEscancianoIchimuraNeweyRobins2022LRO gives general LRO GMM, not an ATE-specific Riesz-spanning iff classification

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_dml_orth_uniform_v1: primitive two-cell witness for strict weak-overlap studentization separation

structure_flags.label: Introduction
structure_flags.one_line: The required sixth subparagraph, 'How research benefits from the conclusion', is missing.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The common fixed-overlap/common-EIF baseline is already present in eid_dml_orth_uniform_v1's fixed-overlap equivalence sanity theorem.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The DML AIPW and ADML Riesz-score ATE EIF baseline is already covered by ChernozhukovEtAl2018ECTJ and ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022ADML.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: V_J and the LRO influence function are not mathematically defined from the published LRO moment/Jacobian/sandwich formulas; Assumption lro-projection imposes the projection object the conjecture is supposed to classify.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The quantifiers drift between a given law P,J and existence of a separate two-cell law; B_J and alpha0 are law-dependent, so the witness statement needs explicit quantification.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Define the actual ATE-specialized LRO moment, Jacobian normalization, projection subspace, influence function, and sandwich variance map J -> V_J before stating the iff frontier.
Restate Conjecture 2 as an explicit existence theorem over J and a two-cell law, with cell probabilities, propensities, and residual variances pinned enough to make K_J > 0 auditable.
Add the missing Introduction paragraph explaining how research benefits from the conclusion.
Demote Theorem 1 to a calibration lemma/proposition or explicitly label it as already-known, citing Hahn 1998 or Hirano-Imbens-Ridder 2003 alongside the DML/ADML papers for the ATE EIF baseline.

recommended_next_step: Revise the structure and make the LRO report map explicit; the flagship novelty can survive, but it cannot rest on the projection convention as an assumption.
